subject: [PATCH v4 02/11] mm: add PMD swap entry splitting support

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch adds support for splitting a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries in the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) subsystem. The new code handles the case where a huge page is split due to a non-present PMD, and it preserves flags such as soft_dirty, uffd_wp, and exclusive when creating the new PTEs. The patch also includes a warning to prevent future callers from breaking an invariant that assumes the page and folio are uninitialized in this branch. This change is part of a larger series of patches aimed at improving memory management for huge pages.
